research_series.series_name: Metabolic Fatigue Clusters,Glucose Variability,Metabolic Flexibility,Exercise Intolerance,and Brain Fog

mediator.name: impaired_oxidative_phosphorylation_or_fat_oxidation
mediator.pathway: reduced ATP generation and abnormal lactate/fat oxidation response to exertion
mediator.mechanism: impaired mitochondrial function and poor metabolic flexibility increase exertional intolerance and PEM risk

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: When fatigue worsens after exertion with abnormal CPET/lactate/fat-oxidation signatures, prioritize metabolic energy/recovery branch over generic tiredness.

hypotheses.reason: CPET, muscle biopsy and metabolomics support impaired oxidative metabolism and lactate/fat oxidation abnormalities in ME/CFS/long COVID.

evidence.summary: Strongest evidence is for mitochondrial dysfunction/impaired fat oxidation driving exertional intolerance, fatigue and PEM. Glycemic variability predicts long-term cognitive/adverse outcomes, but acute postprandial brain fog remains less proven.

key_sources.title: Differential Characterization of Metabolic Fatigue Clusters
key_sources.relevant_finding: Mitochondrial dysfunction causes exertional intolerance/fatigue/PEM; high glycemic variability predicts cognitive/adverse outcomes; postprandial brain fog chain unclear.
key_sources.population: ME/CFS, long COVID, diabetes/metabolic syndrome, athletes
key_sources.limitations: Direct acute postprandial neurobehavioral chains remain sparse.

limitations: GV more established for long-term outcomes than acute symptoms
Mental fatigue can impair endurance through perception without metabolic marker change

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Acute postprandial glucose spikes directly cause brain fog outside diabetes
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Direct CGM+cognitive testing evidence is limited; many studies measure glycemic response without neurobehavioral outcomes.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: Initial phenotype node when fatigue appears meal/exertion/metabolism-related but mechanism is unclear.
Disproportionate lactate response at low exertional load; possible marker of impaired metabolic handling.

research_gaps: Direct acute postprandial glycemic excursion→attention/fatigue studies outside diabetes
Mechanistic intervention trials restoring mitochondrial function

most_supported_patterns: Mitochondrial dysfunction/impaired fat oxidation drives exertional symptoms
High glycemic variability predicts long-term cognitive/adverse outcomes
patterns_requiring_caution: brain FDG-PET hypometabolism as universal explanation
recommended_next_queries: Final graph guardrails and validation protocol
summary.overall_assessment: Strong for exertional/mitochondrial cluster; cautious for acute postprandial brain fog.

works_cited.title: Differential Characterization of Metabolic Fatigue Clusters
